title: Agile Maturity Scan
lead: A structured assessment that measures agile maturity across teams, leadership, and delivery — producing a prioritised improvement roadmap.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: The Agile Maturity Scan is a diagnostic instrument that determines where an organisation stands on the Agile adoption curve. It examines practices, culture, tooling, and governance across teams and leadership to produce a scored baseline. The output drives informed decisions about where to invest in Agile coaching, training, and process improvement — and provides the evidence base for designing an Agile-oriented IT Operating Model.

sections.heading: ITOM Perspective
sections.body: An Agile Maturity Scan is typically one of the first steps in an ITOM engagement where the target state includes Agile ways of working. Without a clear picture of current maturity, transformation plans are built on assumptions. The scan anchors ITOM design decisions — which teams can move to autonomous delivery now, where coaching is needed first, and what governance changes are realistic in the short term. It prevents over-designing for a maturity level the organisation has not yet reached.

items.desc: sprint execution, ceremony quality, backlog management, definition of done
servant leadership, psychological safety, tolerance for experimentation and failure
clarity of vision, stakeholder engagement, prioritisation effectiveness
cycle time, WIP limits, release frequency, dependency management
cross-team coordination, programme-level ceremonies, portfolio alignment
board hygiene, CI/CD maturity, metrics visibility
Maturity score per dimension (1–5 scale, team and organisational level)
Strengths and critical gaps with supporting evidence
Prioritised improvement actions — quick wins and structural changes
Input for ITOM target design and Agile transformation roadmap
structured sessions with teams, product owners, scrum masters, and leadership
standardised questionnaire providing quantitative maturity scores
attendance at team ceremonies (sprint planning, retrospective, review) for direct observation
scores positioned against industry reference data and Atos client base
